desc.line_1: An open source agentic content system.
desc.line_2: Capture content, shape context, and run agents on top of it.

What.title: What's Polywise?
What.subtitle: An Evolving System, Not a Record

0.title: Agent Group
0.desc:
Each agent has its own private prompts, tools, skills, and knowledge graph to activate personalized capabilities.
By assembling agents into groups, Polywise can deliver distinct SOPs and collaborative workflows for the user.

1.title: FST
1.desc:
Full Self Thinking is a harness architecture built for effectively unbounded conversations within a single session.
It adds a dynamic sliding window, shadow context injection, and background sub-agents that asynchronously extract key information back into context.

2.title: Pipeline
2.desc:
Polywise offers full-text retrieval, vector retrieval, graph retrieval with depth-based iterative traversal, and hybrid retrieval.
Entity relationships are built as content is ingested, so AI can decide which retrieval path to use without asking users to wire relationships by hand.

3.title: Callback
3.desc:
Accepted knowledge increases the weight of key nodes and search edges inside the graph.
Matched content is also reinforced, so the next retrieval cycle ranks proven knowledge higher.

4.title: Post-Think
4.desc:
A background post-thinking loop actively reads recent messages and distills valuable information into knowledge.
It is an ongoing knowledge distillation process that makes the system understand you better over time.

5.title: Rewire
5.desc:
Inspired by Hebbian learning, Rewire prunes the knowledge graph so concepts activated together become more strongly connected.
The core of memory is not only remembering but also forgetting, and the point of recording is not storage alone but becoming part of the model's thinking context.

0.title: Stateless AI
0.pain: Every new session starts cold, so you keep re-explaining the same work.
0.solution:
Polywise turns saved content into reusable context.
Agents can retrieve scoped knowledge instead of depending on whatever still fits in the current chat window.

1.title: Fragmented context
1.pain: Tasks, notes, links, projects, and agent prompts usually live in separate tools.
1.solution:
Polywise keeps content, sessions, todos, projects, and agents in one local system.
That makes context easier to connect, search, reuse, and automate.

2.title: Cloud lock-in
2.pain: Many AI workflows run on opaque systems you cannot inspect, migrate, or truly own.
2.solution:
Polywise keeps the system open source and model-flexible.
You can bring providers, local models, and custom tools without giving up ownership of the underlying system.

Features.title: Features
Features.subtitle: Core capabilities for context, retrieval, and execution

0.title: Posts
0.desc:
Write structured posts with outline navigation, local editing, and saved drafts.
Turn raw notes into readable long-form content inside the app.

1.title: Groups
1.desc:
Organize agents into shared groups and compare their responses in one thread.
Coordinate multiple specialists around the same prompt instead of isolated chats.

2.title: Projects
2.desc:
Bind sessions to projects and keep files, context, and outputs beside the working tree.
Review agent results while staying anchored to the codebase or folder that matters.

3.title: IM Integration
3.desc:
Connect bots like WeChat or Discord and manage shared runtime settings from one place.
Route agent workflows into the channels where your team already communicates.

0.a: Polywise is an open source agentic content system. It helps you capture content, build reusable context, and run agents against that context instead of starting from an empty chat every time.

1.a: You can save content, organize it by project or agent, search it through the retrieval pipeline, and then run sessions, tasks, or scheduled jobs that use that context.

2.a: No. Writing is one use case, but the system is built for broader agentic work: research, task execution, workflow coordination, link capture, and long-running project context.

3.q: Do I need extra AI subscriptions to use Polywise?
3.a: Not always. What you need depends on which models or providers you want to run. Polywise is designed to work with different providers rather than forcing one billing path.

4.q: Can I use my existing model providers with Polywise?
4.a: Yes. Polywise is built around provider flexibility, so you can connect the model stack that already fits your workflow instead of switching everything to a bundled vendor.

5.a: Yes. Polywise includes support for local runtimes such as llama-based setups, so part of the system can stay fully on your machine when you want tighter control.

6.a: Polywise uses a hybrid retrieval pipeline. It combines keywords, vectors, recall, and reranking so agents can recover relevant context instead of relying on a single search method.

7.a: Polywise is open source. Your content, memory, and runtime state remain inspectable and under your control, which makes backups, inspection, and privacy boundaries much clearer than cloud-only systems.

8.a: Yes. Polywise includes sessions, todos, schedules, link fetch jobs, and connector surfaces that can be composed into recurring workflows instead of manual one-off runs.

9.a: Yes. Polywise is available on GitHub, which means you can inspect the code, track changes, and understand the system you are building your workflow on.
